ad_text: What is raspberry blowing? It’s when babies press their lips together and blow air out, making a buzzing sound (sometimes with bubbles!). It’s also called a lip trill. Those adorable raspberries are your baby’s first steps toward speech development 💬✨ Why do babies do it? ✔️ They’re exploring how their mouth works ✔️ It helps strengthen the muscles used for speech ✔️ They love the cause-and-effect of making a sound ✔️ It can be a fun way to play, self-soothe, or get your attention When does it happen? Usually around 4 to 6 months, though it can vary. It’s a great sign your baby is experimenting with sound and building coordination 👶💛 Share this with a mama who needs to read this! #johnsonsbaby #purelove #babycare #love #raspberryblowing
competitor_why: Massive baby-care incumbent with strong parent-trust branding and constant creative presence.
